name: Rye bread (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (as-eaten; ~37 g water, ~63 g dry matter)

components.name: starch
components.source: USDA FDC 172684 (available carbohydrate 48.3 - fiber 5.8 - total sugars 3.8)
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source: gelatinised rye/wheat starch; hydrolysed by microbial amylases to maltose/glucose

components.name: maltose
components.source: USDA FDC 172684 total sugars 3.8; proportion from scald-rye HPLC (PMC11941048, maltose 12.1-13.3% dw >> other sugars)
components.evidence: DOMINANT free sugar in leavened rye bread - yeast/LAB deplete glucose/fructose but leave maltose from amylolysis; amount apportioned within measured USDA total sugars

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 172684 total sugars; ordering from PMC11941048 (fructose 0.37-0.89% dw) & FODMAP PMC8074121
components.evidence: partly consumed during fermentation (heterofermentative LAB reduce fructose to mannitol)

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 172684 total sugars; ordering from PMC11941048 (glucose 0.43-0.56% dw)
components.evidence: low residual - rapidly fermented

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 172684 total sugars; PMC11941048 (sucrose 0.30-0.50% dw)
components.evidence: residual/added sugar

components.name: mannitol
components.evidence: heterofermentative-LAB fermentation product of fructose; present in sourdough rye, fermentable carbon source; not quantified in final baked bread

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: Scald rye bread PMC11941048 (0.81-2.06 g/100g dw); LAB rye PMC8759695; sourdough survey (0.22-0.87 g/100g fw)
components.evidence: MISSING from FooDB record - dominant sourdough organic acid; major fermentable carbon source and defining metabolite of rye bread

components.name: acetic acid
components.source: Scald rye bread PMC11941048 (0.16-0.23 g/100g dw); sourdough survey (0.07-0.10 g/100g fw)
components.evidence: MISSING from FooDB record - heterofermentative-LAB product; fermentation quotient lactic:acetic ~4-10:1

components.name: succinic acid
components.source: Scald rye bread PMC11941048 (0.03-0.17 g/100g dw)
components.evidence: MISSING from FooDB record - minor fermentation acid

components.name: dietary fiber (arabinoxylan + beta-glucan)
components.evidence: rye is arabinoxylan-rich; largely non-fermentable by most single strains but degradable by specialist glycoside hydrolases

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: N source; amino-acid profile below

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: dominant amino acid (gluten-derived Glx)

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: replaces FooDB 'D-Serine' artifact - protein Ser is the L-enantiomer

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: limiting amino acid in rye/cereal protein

components.name: sodium
components.evidence: from added salt - high

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: P source (partly phytate-bound, released by sourdough phytase)

components.name: iron
components.evidence: single consolidated Fe value (FooDB listed Fe2+/Fe3+/'Fe2+ mitochondria' redundantly)

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: notably high in rye/cereal

components.name: folate (B9)
components.evidence: total folate (incl. fortification/enrichment)

components.name: vitamin C (ascorbate)
components.evidence: trace (sometimes added as dough improver)

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: dominant PUFA of rye/wheat lipid

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: dominant MUFA (total MUFA 1.312 g)

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: dominant SFA (total SFA 0.625 g)

components.name: ferulic acid
components.evidence: genuine - rye is exceptionally ferulic-acid-rich (bound to arabinoxylan); kept (present in BiGG as ferulate)

remove.name: Cyanocobalamin
remove.reason: vitamin B12 = 0 in unfortified plant/cereal bread (USDA FDC 172684 B12 = 0); not synthesised by rye

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed vitamin A (animal-derived) = 0 in rye bread (USDA vitamin A = 0); implausible

remove.name: Beta-Carotene
remove.reason: USDA vitamin A / carotene = 0 for rye bread; negligible/absent, FooDB predicted

remove.name: Beta-Cryptoxanthin
remove.reason: carotenoid absent in rye bread (USDA vitamin A = 0); FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Fe2+ mitochondria
remove.reason: model-compartment artifact - duplicate of iron; a food has no mitochondrial Fe pool

remove.name: Iron (Fe3+)
remove.reason: redundant duplicate iron entry; consolidated into single 'iron' value

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: non-biological enantiomer; protein-derived serine is L-serine (replaced by L-serine)

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient of bread

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace element; not a quantified rye-bread nutrient, FooDB noise

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: free cobalt ion - ultratrace/contaminant; no B12 pathway in plant bread to make it a nutrient

remove.name: Vaccenic acid
remove.reason: 18:1 n-7 trans/ruminant fatty acid; implausible in cereal lipid, FooDB predicted

remove.name: Gadoleic acid
remove.reason: 20:1 fatty acid; not a component of rye/wheat lipid (USDA reports no 20:1), FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Hexanoate (n-C6:0)
remove.reason: medium-chain fatty acid absent in cereal lipid; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Octanoic acid
remove.reason: C8:0 medium-chain fatty acid absent in rye/wheat lipid; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Nonanoate
remove.reason: C9:0 fatty acid; not a rye-bread lipid component, FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Decanoic acid
remove.reason: C10:0 medium-chain fatty acid absent in cereal lipid; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: Dodecanoic acid
remove.reason: lauric acid (C12:0) absent in rye/wheat lipid; FooDB predicted noise

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: furanocoumarin of Apiaceae/citrus; biologically implausible in rye bread, FooDB mis-assignment

missing_added: lactic acid (dominant sourdough organic acid, ~0.6 g/100g) - absent from FooDB record
acetic acid (~0.15 g/100g) - absent from FooDB record
succinic acid (~0.05 g/100g) - absent from FooDB record
maltose apportioned as the dominant free sugar (~2.5 g/100g within USDA total sugars 3.8)
quantitative amounts for all sugars, amino acids, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids from USDA FDC 172684
mannitol (rye-sourdough LAB fermentation product; present but unquantified in baked bread)
explicit starch amount (~38.7 g/100g) as the dominant carbon source
L-serine to replace the D-serine artifact
keep_uncovered: ferulic acid, p-coumaric acid, syringic acid, caffeic acid - genuine rye phenolic acids
maltodextrin - genuine starch-hydrolysis product (subsumed by starch/maltose for growth)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 172684 (Bread, rye) - full nutrient panel: macros, sugars, 18 amino acids, minerals, vitamins, fatty acids

Scald fermentation rye bread, Foods/Nutrients 2025, PMC11941048 - sugars (maltose 12-13% dw, glucose/fructose/sucrose) and organic acids (lactic 0.81-2.06, acetic 0.16-0.23, succinic 0.03-0.17 g/100g dw)
LAB starter wholemeal rye bread, PMC8759695 - lactic/acetic acid and fermentation quotient
FODMAP fingerprinting of bakery products & sourdoughs, PMC8074121 - fructose, FOS/fructans, mannitol in rye and rye sourdough
Sourdough organic-acid survey ranges (lactic 0.22-0.87, acetic 0.07-0.10 g/100g fw)
FooDB FOOD00809 (Rye bread) - base record and phenolic/alkylresorcinol coverage
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources are STARCH (~39 g/100g) and its hydrolysis product MALTOSE (~2.5 g/100g, the main residual free sugar after yeast/LAB fermentation); LACTIC ACID (~0.6 g/100g, added here - was missing) is the defining sourdough metabolite and a key organic-acid carbon source, with acetic and succinic acids from heterofermentative LAB. Sugar split is anchored to the measured USDA total sugars (3.8 g/100g) with maltose-dominant proportions from rye-bread HPLC literature; organic acids on fresh-weight basis converted from dry-weight fermentation studies (rye bread ~63% dry matter). Removed FooDB noise: absent animal/plant-implausible vitamins (B12, retinol, carotenes), ultratrace contaminants (Ni, Cr, Co, Fe compartment duplicate), non-cereal fatty acids (medium-chain C6-C12, 20:1, 18:1 n-7), and the D-serine enantiomer artifact.
